description: Desktop-style menu bar (File / Edit / View...). The root is role="menubar"; each MenubarTrigger is role="menuitem" that opens a role="menu" dropdown. Menus support sub-menus, checkbox items, radio items, labels, and separators, all with correct ARIA wiring via Radix.

keyboard.action: Moves focus between top-level menu triggers.
Opens the focused menu and moves focus to its first item.
Within an open menu, moves between items.
Opens a submenu when the focused item is a SubTrigger.
Closes the current submenu (or moves to the previous top-level menu if already at the menu's root).
Closes the open menu and returns focus to its trigger.
Moves focus out of the menubar entirely.
Type-ahead jumps to the first item whose label starts with that letter.

focus.notes: Roving tabindex: only one trigger in the menubar is a tab stop at a time. While a menu is open focus stays inside it; closing returns focus to the relevant trigger.

announcements: The root announces as a menu bar with its item count.
Each trigger announces with the "menu" popup affordance.
Items announce with their role, label, checked state, and submenu indicator where applicable.
screenReader.notes: MenubarShortcut renders a decorative label for keyboard shortcuts. The consumer must register the actual hotkey separately; Menubar only shows the hint.

contrast.text: Trigger text uses foreground; focused / open state uses text-accent-foreground on bg-accent. Both meet WCAG AA
contrast.ui: Separator uses bg-muted; focus highlight meets AA non-text contrast

motion.reducedMotion: Dropdown content uses data-[state=open]:animate-in with fade + slide utilities. Gate with motion-reduce:animate-none if strict compliance matters.

consumerResponsibilities: Use Menubar only in app-like interfaces (e.g. editors, dashboards). For a simple site nav bar, NavigationMenu or a plain nav with links is more appropriate.
Register keyboard shortcuts globally; MenubarShortcut is a visual hint, not a binding.
Keep labels short and match common desktop conventions (File, Edit, View) so users can predict contents.
